title: Helsinki Animal Law Conference 2026: “Reimagining Justice Across Species”
content:
Helsinki Animal Law Conference 2026: “Reimagining Justice Across Species”

txrissan@helsinki.fi Fri, 17/04/2026 - 14:00
We are pleased to announce the annual conference of the Helsinki Animal Law Centre in collaboration with the Legacy and Rebound projects. The conference will be held in Helsinki from June 9-10, 2026.

title: RADIA Seminar: Gender, Misogyny and Extremism
content:
RADIA Seminar: Gender, Misogyny and Extremism

henrikal@helsinki.fi Fri, 17/04/2026 - 11:11
RADIA hosts a seminar examining the growing intersections between gender, misogyny, and extremism. The programme includes a keynote by Dr. Elizabeth Pearson and presentations by Docent Katarina Pettersson and Academy Research Fellow Sanna Tirkkonen.

title: Suolistosta imeytyvä kolesteroli voi lisätä vakavien sydäntapahtumien riskiä
content:
Effective cholesterol absorption from the intestine may increase the risk of serious cardiac events

purra@helsinki.fi Thu, 16/04/2026 - 11:42
The LDL cholesterol level alone does not indicate whether a person will develop heart disease. Some people absorb cholesterol particularly effectively from the intestine. This may increase the risk of serious cardiac events even if they are not observed in blood cholesterol levels.

content:
Seminar Day on Well-being, Motivation, and Learning

archakna@helsinki.fi Wed, 15/04/2026 - 19:58
The Helsinki SEN research community organised a full-day seminar with three guest speakers: Professor Antti-Tuomas Pulkka from the National Defence University as the keynote speaker and two visiting guest presenters from the U.S., Elizabeth Ko and Marc Noble.

content:
Addressing supply chain resilience and cross-border climate effects in the Nordic countries

sailatoi@helsinki.fi Wed, 15/04/2026 - 14:19
The Urban Environmental Policy research group at the University of Helsinki is contributing to a Nordic research project on transboundary climate risks. The project examines how Nordic countries can better respond to climate impacts that originate beyond the region.

title: A word path to sustainability: Meg Holden on translanguaging for urban transformations
content:
A word path to sustainability: Meg Holden on translanguaging for urban transformations

miur@helsinki.fi Wed, 15/04/2026 - 09:54
On 8th of April, we were honored to host Urbaria Guest Lecture with Professor Meg Holden from Simon Fraser University, Vancouver. She discussed how translanguaging is researched and why it matters in urban transformations.

title: Varför röstar finlandssvenskar som de gör? – Nytt projekt kartlägger språkminoriteters väljarbeteende
content:
Why do Finland’s Swedish speakers vote as they do? New project examines voting habits of linguistic minorities

laok@helsinki.fi Wed, 15/04/2026 - 16:38
For the first time, the political behaviour of Finland’s Swedish speakers (or Finland-Swedes) will be studied in depth and compared with that of similar linguistic minorities across Europe. The goal is to understand what truly drives voting habits.

title: Arkadiankadun siltaremontti aiheuttaa muutoksia liikennejärjestelyihin Luonnontieteellisen museon lähistöllä
content:
Renewal of Arkadiankatu Bridge will cause changes to traffic arrangements near the Natural History Museum

johanemi@helsinki.fi Wed, 07/01/2026 - 08:40
The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

The renovation will start with the construction of temporary traffic arrangements from Wednesday 7 January onwards. The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.
